title: Dry cell weight by centrifugation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c8og5","text":"This protocols describe the steps required for dry cell weight measurements using microcentrifugal tubes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ftd2p","text":"Cell density for harvesting is important. The final mass weighted need to be inside the linear range of the balance. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bu56g","text":"All steps described in this protocol are intended to be conducted in a research laboratory. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Analytical balance with high precision (The higher the precision the better. For example a balance with a 0.1mg readability, could account to approximately 10% error alone in a measurement of 1mL sample of a culture at 1g/L) Microcentrifugal tubes Microcentrifuge
Label microcentrifugal tubes Dry the tubes at 90 °C , Overnight Cool tubes at Room temperature for 00:30:00 Record the weight of the tubes

Harvest 2 mL of culture in a previously weighted tube Centrifuge the sample at 20000 rcf, 25 °C, 1 minute Carefully remove the supernatant by pipetting Wash the cells with ddH20, and centrifuge the sample at 20000 rcf, 25 °C, 1 minute Carefully remove the supernatant by pipetting Dry the tubes at 90 °C , Overnight Cool tubes at Room temperature for 00:30:00 Record the weight of the tubes Subtract the initial tube weight to achieve the dry cell weight
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
